item.title: StarTalk on the Future of Space Travel
item.description: StarTalk explores when space travel may become more accessible to more people.

media:content.media:title: StarTalk on the Future of Space Travel
media:content.media:description: StarTalk explores when space travel may become more accessible to more people.

item.title: StarTalk: The Sun’s True Color
item.description: StarTalk explores why the sun’s color is often seen differently than yellow.

media:content.media:title: StarTalk: The Sun’s True Color
media:content.media:description: StarTalk explores why the sun’s color is often seen differently than yellow.

item.title: Are We Intelligent Enough? | StarTalk
item.description: StarTalk explores the question: are we intelligent enough?

media:content.media:title: Are We Intelligent Enough? | StarTalk
media:content.media:description: StarTalk explores the question: are we intelligent enough?

item.title: StarTalk asks: Are we alone in the universe?
item.description: StarTalk explores a big question: would discovering aliens or learning we’re alone be more surprising?

media:content.media:title: StarTalk asks: Are we alone in the universe?
media:content.media:description: StarTalk explores a big question: would discovering aliens or learning we’re alone be more surprising?

item.title: How Earth Would Look as a Schoolroom Globe
item.description: StarTalk explores how Earth might appear if it were represented as a schoolroom globe.

media:content.media:title: How Earth Would Look as a Schoolroom Globe
media:content.media:description: StarTalk explores how Earth might appear if it were represented as a schoolroom globe.

item.title: How Would a Time Machine Work?
item.description: StarTalk explores the idea of how a time machine might work.

media:content.media:title: How Would a Time Machine Work?
media:content.media:description: StarTalk explores the idea of how a time machine might work.

item.title: StarTalk on how the skeleton produces antimatter
item.description: StarTalk explains a surprising science fact: your skeleton can produce antimatter naturally.

media:content.media:title: StarTalk on how the skeleton produces antimatter
media:content.media:description: StarTalk explains a surprising science fact: your skeleton can produce antimatter naturally.

item.title: StarTalk on a new trade route opening
item.description: StarTalk explores how climate change has opened a new trade route, highlighting a real-world shift in global shipping.

media:content.media:title: StarTalk on a new trade route opening
media:content.media:description: StarTalk explores how climate change has opened a new trade route, highlighting a real-world shift in global shipping.

item.title: Do we understand the speed of light?
item.description: StarTalk explores a classic question about the speed of light and what it means for our understanding of the universe.

media:content.media:title: Do we understand the speed of light?
media:content.media:description: StarTalk explores a classic question about the speed of light and what it means for our understanding of the universe.

item.title: What color is outer space?
item.description: StarTalk explores the question of what color outer space appears to be.

media:content.media:title: What color is outer space?
media:content.media:description: StarTalk explores the question of what color outer space appears to be.

item.title: StarTalk on solar power and the future of energy
item.description: Neil deGrasse Tyson and Charles Liu discuss solar power, renewable resources, and the challenges of moving to new technology.

media:content.media:title: StarTalk on solar power and the future of energy
media:content.media:description: Neil deGrasse Tyson and Charles Liu discuss solar power, renewable resources, and the challenges of moving to new technology.

item.title: Why astronauts feel weightless in space
item.description: StarTalk explains why astronauts feel weightless in space.

media:content.media:description: StarTalk explains why astronauts feel weightless in space.

item.title: StarTalk: Missions Returning Us to the Moon
item.description: StarTalk explores the missions helping bring us back to the Moon.
